description_long: This is a large, two-story log cabin with a prominent gabled roof and multiple intersecting rooflines, constructed primarily from natural wood logs and stone. The exterior features a spacious covered porch with wooden railings, stone steps, and a stone foundation, giving it a rustic yet sturdy appearance. The roof is dark and shingled, with deep overhangs and exposed timber beams, adding to the architectural detail. Windows are distributed asymmetrically, with a mix of small and medium rectangular panes, some grouped and others standalone, providing ample natural light. The cabin sits on a slightly raised grassy plot, with the terrain sloping gently around the structure. Visible details include a chimney, porch furniture, and a stairway leading up to the main entrance.
description: A large, rustic log cabin with a dark gabled roof, stone foundation, and a covered porch with wooden railings. The structure features exposed timber beams, asymmetrical windows, and sits on a grassy, slightly sloped plot.
description_view_0: Side view showing the log construction, stone foundation, and a small porch area with visible railings and steps.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the deep roof overhang, exposed beams, and the main covered porch with outdoor furniture.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the main entrance, wide porch with steps, and a symmetrical arrangement of windows and railings.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back porch, additional windows, and the continuation of the log and stone construction.
attribute_roof_type: complex gable roof with multiple intersecting gables, deep overhangs, and dark shingle cladding
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical distribution of small to medium rectangular windows, some grouped and others standalone, with no clear repetitive pattern
attribute_door_placement: one main entrance door centered on the front porch, with additional doors visible on the side and rear porches
